title: Dinas (Wales) via Wroxeter & Tallybont (Whitsun)

main_route.excerpt: We set off just before lunch & ate at Wroxeter, the remains of a Roman town, then drove on to Dinas.

main_route.normalized_name: Dinas, near Llanbedr, Gwynedd, Wales, UK
main_route.excerpt:
then drove on to Dinas. Handbedr 04341 23585
	We had to wait for a "mob camp" to finish a game with a very hard ball, before finally parking the van.

geocode_hint.hint: near Llanbedr, Gwynedd; old phone prefix 04341 = Llanbedr exchange

excursions.label: Sunday: church at Tal-y-bont, barbeque with Hudfords, beach with Thomas' boat

stops.excerpt: On the Sunday we went to church at 11.30. at Tallybont, where the service started off with a lay reader & the vicar arrived just in time for the sermon.

stops.normalized_name: beach near Llanbedr, Gwynedd, Wales, UK
stops.excerpt: went to the beach for the boys to play on Thomas' boat. There were pools for it, with the tide coming in.

geocode_hint.hint: tidal beach near Llanbedr/Tal-y-bont, Gwynedd; possibly Shell Island area or Llanbedr beach

summary: A Whitsun family camping trip from Birmingham to a campsite at Dinas near Llanbedr in Gwynedd, Wales, stopping at the Roman ruins of Wroxeter on the way. On the Sunday the family attended church at Tal-y-bont, hosted the Hudfords for a barbeque, and went to the nearby tidal beach where the boys sailed Thomas' boat.
extraction_notes: The diary covers a single page (A_0041_R). 'Dinas' is identified as near Llanbedr by the phone number annotation 'Handbedr 04341 23585' (Llanbedr old STD code 04341). The end date is not recorded in the surviving page; the trip likely lasted the Whitsun long weekend (25–27 May 1991). Tallybont is rendered as 'Tallybont' in the diary; normalised to 'Tal-y-bont'. 'The Hudfords' are family friends whose exact identity is unknown. No ferry or other sea crossing involved — entirely within Great Britain.
